title: [spec-compliance] SenML location uses wrong unit string vs spec
description: spec/appendix-senml.md:33-45 specifies u:"lat" for latitude record and u:"lon" for longitude (per RFC 8428 unit registry for geographic coordinates). However, lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c:335-340 hardcodes "deg" in senml_add_float(..., "deg", ...) for both. senml.h:57-62 defines SENML_LOCATION_LAT="lat", SENML_LOCATION_LON="lon", SENML_LOCATION_UNIT_DEG="deg" but these are not used in encode_location(). coap_location.c:60 calls it. This is a code/spec mismatch on constants. Last touched in project-LICHEN-rgpx:fix (7740e390f) and related bridge-zephyr board configs. MUST align implementation with published spec example and test vectors.
close_reason: Implemented spec-compliant SenML location units using defined constants in both C and Python implementations; aligned with appendix-senml.md example and test expectations. Commit a435259
